title: Mastering the Market Cycle
subtitle: Getting the Odds on Your Side
author: Howard Marks
themes: The market pendulum: investor psychology swings between irreconcilable extremes
Risk is the probability of permanent capital loss, not volatility or standard deviation
Cycles are causal chains, not random fluctuations; each phase sets up the next
Regression to the mean is the self-correcting mechanism that generates cycle returns
Credit cycles are the most volatile and consequential of all market cycles
Being average is rare — 1970-2016, S&P 500 fell within 2% of average only 3 times
Highest risk is perceived lowest risk; lowest risk is perceived highest risk
Positioning and aggressiveness should shift based on cycle location, not forecasting
Distressed debt opportunities emerge at the slammed-shut phase of the credit cycle
Success sows the seeds of failure through complacency, crowding, and overvaluation
We may never know where we are going, but we must understand where we are
Contrarianism requires the willingness to appear wrong while the market becomes more extreme
awards: Foreword by Ben Bernanke (former Chairman, Federal Reserve)
excerpt: Howard Marks, co-founder of Oaktree Capital, applies four decades of institutional investment experience to the single question that most investors get wrong: not where are we going, but where are we? Drawing on economic history, credit cycles, and the psychology of crowds, he offers a framework — not a timing mechanism — for sensing where the pendulum currently stands and positioning accordingly. The market does not move in straight lines; neither should your portfolio.
